# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a multi-service application container that runs a web server (port 80), API server (port 8080), database (port 5432), and Redis (port 6379). Expose all necessary ports so the services can communicate. This is for our all-in-one development container.
# Category: container_security

# Use an official base image
FROM ubuntu:latest

# Install necessary packages
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y \
    apache2 \
    nginx \
    postgresql \
    redis-server \
    curl \
    git \
    python3 \
    python3-pip \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Configure services to run in the background
RUN service apache2 stop && \
    service nginx stop && \
    service postgresql stop && \
    service redis-server stop

# Expose necessary ports
EXPOSE 80 8080 5432 6379

# Set working directory
WORKDIR /app

# Copy application files into the container
COPY . /app

# Install Python dependencies
RUN pip3 install -r requirements.txt

# Start services in the background
CMD service apache2 start && \
    service nginx start && \
    service postgresql start && \
    service redis-server start && \
    python3 app.py &
